summary: httpOnly Cookie Authentication Migration - CRITICAL testing complete. Backend 16/16 tests passed. Frontend login/logout/navigation all working. Fixed critical bug in Users.js (filteredUsers undefined).

issues: Console warning: '<span> cannot be a child of <tbody>' - React DevTools overlay issue, does not affect functionality

critical_code_review_comments: httpOnly cookie authentication working correctly - token stored in secure cookie, not localStorage
Middleware correctly accepts token from both cookie (priority) and Authorization header (transition mode)
Logout properly clears the cookie and protected endpoints return 401 after logout
Frontend correctly stores only user info in localStorage (for UI state), not the token
CORS configured with credentials=True for cookie support
Cookie attributes correct: HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=lax, Max-Age=86400
context_for_next_testing_agent: httpOnly cookie authentication migration is complete and working. The token is now stored in a secure httpOnly cookie instead of localStorage. The middleware supports both cookie and header auth for backward compatibility during transition. Frontend stores only user info in localStorage for UI state. All protected endpoints (/api/services, /api/users, /api/reportes, /api/reportes/estadisticas) work with cookie auth. Logout clears the cookie correctly.

backend: POST /api/auth/login - sets httpOnly cookie with correct attributes (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite=lax)
GET /api/auth/me - returns current user with cookie auth
Middleware accepts both cookie (priority) and header auth (transition mode)
Protected endpoints return 401 without valid auth
frontend: Login form works and redirects to dashboard
Token NOT stored in localStorage (correct - in httpOnly cookie)
User info stored in localStorage for UI state
Navigation to all protected pages works (Dashboard, Usuarios, Servicios, Reportes, Inventario, Tipos de Servicios)
Logout clears user data from localStorage
Protected pages redirect to login after logout

bug_fixes_applied.issue: ReferenceError: filteredUsers is not defined - caused Users page to crash
bug_fixes_applied.fix: Added filteredUsers variable definition using users.filter() with search and role filtering logic
